title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Erlie and Professor Adler discuss the background of Krulmit (also known as Crocetta), noting his history of small businesses in America. Four years prior in Chattanooga, Tennessee, Crocetta teamed up with a man named Harkness (or Stray) to manage a "sun cult" selling "sun pills" made of cod liver oil. Following the Alamo murder, Crocetta and Van's servant, Klint, disappeared without a trace. Erlie notes that while Crocetta investigated a potential alliance between himself and Klint, he found nothing.

The discussion shifts to Alva Strach, a famous Egyptologist who suffered heatstroke in the Valley of the Kings and is believed to be a harmless madman. Although the Hanover County District Attorney believes Strach is innocent of murdering Andrew Van, the evidence points toward Wilga Crosse as the killer. Erlie argues that the true key to the case is identifying the real identity of Wilga Crosetti, suggesting the person could be anyone, including Krosok, who had previously placed the Cult of the Sun near Bradwood. Erlie also theorizes that the motive might involve superstitions regarding vampires and decapitation, common in Central Europe and the Balkans.

Finally, the two debate the nature of a T-shaped symbol. Erlie believes the *crux commissa* is an "Egyptian cross" based on his research in the *Encyclopaedia Britannica*. Professor Adler corrects him, stating that while T-shaped symbols appear in Egyptian hieroglyphics, the term "Egyptian cross" is a misnomer. Adler explains that the true cross-shaped symbol associated with ancient Egypt is the ankh.
